rulings.Case Title: ZURICH AMERICAN INSURANCE COMPANY VS. SANACT, INC., DBA ROTO-ROOTER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, October 12, 2011, Line 2. PLAINTIFF ZURICH AMERICAN INSURANCE COMPANY MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Plaintiff¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternative motion for summary adjudication is granted as to the sixth, eighth, ninth and thirteenth causes of action in the second amended cross-complaint filed by Sanact and Mr. Wray. Summary judgment is improper because the motion does not disposes of all the claims between the parties. Summary adjudication is proper because paragraph 7 of the cross-complaint alleges that a true and correct copy of the 99 policy is attached to the cross-complaint. The 99 policy includes the form 10 endorsement. Even thought the endorsement is not signed or the blanks filled in, the endorsement is still part of the policy. The endorsement precludes coverage for Mr. Simmons¿ workers¿ compensation case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: HASZ CONTRUCTION, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. TYFOOD MEDIA, INC. A NEW YORK CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Determine Settlement Not In Good Faith
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, October 12, 2011, Line 5. DEFENDANTS NABIEL MUSLEH AND MARTEL TOLER'S Notice Of Motion To Determine Settlement Not In Good Faith. Defendant¿s¿ Motion to Determine Settlement Was Not in Good Faith is denied. The motion is not a proper use of the CCP 877.6 procedure since its clear aim is to preclude Tyfood Media from asserting any defenses based on the settlement in case number 509319. Yet no such defenses are before this court on this motion. When and if Tyfood Media seeks a ruling in the other case regarding the affect of the settlement, Mr. Toler and Mr. Musleh can oppose that motion by making any arguments they believe appropriate. The prevailing party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JACQUELINE RAYFORD, ON BEHALF OF HERSELF AND VS. MOBILITY PLUS TRANSPORTATION, LLC, A CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay All Civil Court Proceedings
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, October 12, 2011, Line 19. DEFENDANTS MOBILITY PLUS TRANSPORTATION, LLC, A CORPORATION AND TECTRANS, INC.'S Notice Of Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay All Civil Court Proceedings. Defendants¿ motion to compel arbitration and stay action is granted. Plaintiff has not shown that the arbitration agreement she signed is substantively unconscionable. The failure to exclude PAGA or UCL claims from the agreement does not render the agreement unconscionable. Nor is there any lack of mutuality. Brown is inapposite since that case dealt with a waiver of a PAGA claim and did not hold that a PAGA claim couldn¿t be arbitrated. To the extent that Cruz has any continuing vitality after Concepcion, which is unclear at the moment, plaintiff¿s UCL claim for injunctive relief can be severed from her other claims and tried in this court after the conclusion of the arbitration. The prevailing party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. = (302/HEK)
